name: Banana Handfuls
recipeInstructions:
This is a good cookie recipe to use up those overripe bananas. It was printed in Women's Day magazine in 1976 as an "On the Go Breakfast" but it's good anytime of day.
1. Cream together the shortening, granulated sugar, packed brown sugar, eggs, and vanilla until fluffy.
2. Stir in the mashed banana and set aside.
3. Mix the remaining ingredients (oats, flour, milk powder, walnuts, coconut if using, salt, baking powder, and baking soda) separately.
4. Gradually stir the dry mixture into the banana mixture.
5. Drop rounded tablespoonfuls of dough onto a greased cookie sheet.
6. Shape each drop into a 3-inch round about 1/2 inch thick.
7. Bake at 350°F for 14 minutes or until the cookies spring back when lightly touched. Enjoy!
